model_narrative: This is an investment and operating plan for a Piceance-basin oil-shale syncrude complex over a multi-decade horizon. Across a sequence of expansion and operating periods we decide how hard to run each mining, retorting, upgrading, disposal, and pollution-control process, how much raw shale, renewable water, and groundwater to buy, how much of each final product to make, and how much to invest in each piece of productive equipment. Water is bought along a rising piecewise cost curve, so we also decide how much to take from each segment of that curve. The goal is to maximize the total discounted profit, which nets period sales revenue against the cost of raw materials, royalties, operating costs, and capital.

sets.name: c
sets.doc: all commodities tracked in the material balances, spanning final saleable products, the various raw materials, intermediate streams, and special accounting rows

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: the final saleable products, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: crs
sets.doc: the raw shale grades that can be purchased, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: crr
sets.doc: the renewable water sources that can be purchased each period, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: crg
sets.doc: the non-renewable groundwater source, purchased once over the whole horizon, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: ci
sets.doc: the intermediate commodities that are produced and consumed internally and must net to zero, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: cc
sets.doc: the canyon disposal-space commodity, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: er
sets.doc: the regulated air-pollution emissions, a subset of the commodities

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the production and disposal processes that can be operated; 'dispose-u' is the mine-backfill (dump) disposal process and the three 'mining-*' processes are the mine-up processes

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the productive units (equipment) that must be installed to support process operation

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the ordered break points of the piecewise water-purchase cost curve, from cheapest (i1) to most expensive (i5)

sets.name: tf
sets.doc: the full ordered set of expansion (investment) time periods, including the initial 1985-89 build period

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the operating time periods in chronological order; a subset of the expansion periods that excludes the initial 1985-89 build period

params.name: a
params.doc: input-output coefficient: net amount of commodity c produced (positive) or consumed (negative) per unit of process p run; missing entries are zero

params.name: b
params.doc: capacity-utilization coefficient: units of productive unit m required per unit of process p run; missing entries are zero

params.name: bd
params.doc: available canyon disposal space for the canyon-space commodity

params.name: bs
params.doc: total recoverable shale available for each shale grade over the whole horizon, in million tons

params.name: pf
params.doc: selling price of each final product in each operating period

params.name: newcap
params.doc: additional syncrude output capacity that may be brought online in each operating period

params.name: bc
params.doc: background ambient concentration of each regulated emission before any project emissions are added

params.name: esa
params.doc: the ambient air-quality standard (allowed concentration ceiling) for each regulated emission

params.name: ps
params.doc: the royalty rate charged per barrel of syncrude produced, in dollars per barrel

vars.name: z
vars.doc: operating level of each process in each expansion period

vars.name: x
vars.doc: output quantity of each final product in each expansion period

vars.name: us
vars.doc: purchased quantity of each shale grade in each expansion period

vars.name: ur
vars.doc: purchased quantity of each renewable water source in each operating period

vars.name: ug
vars.doc: total purchased quantity of groundwater over the whole horizon

vars.name: h
vars.doc: investment (new capacity installed) in each productive unit in each expansion period

vars.name: uur
vars.doc: renewable water purchased on each break-point segment of the cost curve, by source and expansion period

vars.name: uug
vars.doc: groundwater purchased on each break-point segment of the cost curve

vars.name: pi
vars.doc: total discounted profit over the whole horizon, in million dollars

vars.name: r
vars.doc: sales revenue in each operating period

vars.name: phiy
vars.doc: shale royalty cost in each operating period

vars.name: phir
vars.doc: renewable water cost in each operating period

vars.name: phig
vars.doc: total groundwater cost over the whole horizon

vars.name: phik
vars.doc: cost of capital (investment) charged in each operating period

vars.name: phio
vars.doc: operating cost other than raw materials in each operating period

constraints.name: a[cr,
constraints.intent: For each shale grade in each operating period, the net amount of that shale produced across all processes must exactly offset the amount of that shale purchased, so that everything consumed is accounted for by purchases.

constraints.name: a[cr,
constraints.intent: For each renewable water source in each operating period, the net amount of that water produced across all processes must exactly offset the amount of that water purchased that period.

constraints.name: a[cr,
constraints.intent: For groundwater, the net amount produced across all processes summed over every operating period must exactly offset the total groundwater purchased over the whole horizon.

constraints.name: a[cc,
constraints.intent: For each intermediate commodity in each operating period, the net amount produced across all processes must balance to zero, since these streams are neither bought nor sold.

constraints.name: a[cc,
constraints.intent: For each final product in each operating period, the net amount produced across all processes must equal the output quantity recorded for that product that period.

constraints.name: z[pp,
constraints.intent: No mine backfilling is allowed during the first two operating periods, so the level of the backfill disposal process must be zero in each of those two periods.

constraints.name: z[pp,
constraints.intent: In the third operating period, the amount of mine backfilling done cannot exceed a fixed fraction of the amount mined in the first operating period.

constraints.name: z[pp,
constraints.intent: In the fourth operating period, the amount of mine backfilling done cannot exceed a fixed fraction of the total amount mined in the first two operating periods, after deducting whatever backfilling was already done in the third period.

constraints.name: a[cc,
constraints.intent: For canyon disposal space, the total spoils sent to the canyons across all processes and all operating periods, scaled by the length of a period, must not exceed the available canyon space.

constraints.name: us[cr,
constraints.intent: For each shale grade, the total purchased over all operating periods, scaled by the length of a period, must not exceed the recoverable amount of that grade available over the whole horizon.

constraints.name: ur[cr,
constraints.intent: For each renewable water source in each operating period, the total purchased that period must equal the sum of the amounts taken from each segment of that source's piecewise cost curve.

constraints.name: ug[cr]
constraints.intent: For groundwater, the total purchased over the horizon must equal the sum of the amounts taken from each segment of its piecewise cost curve.

constraints.name: a[e,
constraints.intent: For each regulated emission in each operating period, the ambient concentration produced by the project's processes, added to the existing background concentration, must stay within that emission's air-quality standard.

constraints.name: b[mm,
constraints.intent: For each productive unit in each operating period, the capacity needed to run all the processes that period cannot exceed the total capacity that has been installed in that unit through investments made in all earlier expansion periods.

constraints.name: x['syncrude',
constraints.intent: For syncrude output, the amount produced in each operating period cannot exceed the amount produced in the immediately preceding period plus the additional syncrude capacity allowed to come online that period. In the first operating period there is no preceding period to carry over.

constraints.name: r[tt]
constraints.intent: For each operating period, sales revenue is defined as the sum over all final products of that product's selling price times its output quantity.

constraints.name: phiy[tt]
constraints.intent: For each operating period, the shale royalty cost is defined as the royalty rate per barrel times the net syncrude produced across all processes that period.

constraints.name: phir[tt]
constraints.intent: For each operating period, the renewable water cost is defined by charging each segment of each source's piecewise cost curve at its own incremental rate for the amount taken from that segment, normalized by the segment width, and summing over all segments and sources.

constraints.name: phig
constraints.intent: The total groundwater cost is defined by charging each segment of the groundwater piecewise cost curve at its own incremental rate for the amount taken from that segment, normalized by the segment width, and summing over all segments.

constraints.name: phik[tt]
constraints.intent: For each operating period, the cost of capital is defined as a discounting factor times the sum, over every productive unit and every earlier expansion period, of that unit's per-capacity capital charge times the investment made.

constraints.name: phio[tt]
constraints.intent: For each operating period, the other operating cost is defined as the sum over all processes of that process's unit operating cost times its operating level that period.

constraints.name: pi
constraints.intent: Total discounted profit is defined by taking, in each operating period, the sales revenue less the renewable water, operating, royalty, and capital costs, discounting each period to present value and scaling by the period length, summing over all operating periods, and then subtracting the total groundwater cost.

query: We're thinking about limiting how hard we lean on the richest 35-gallon-per-ton retorting line — hold total ret-35 throughput across all periods to 900. What's the profit picture under that limit?
expected_pyomo: model.ret35_total_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z['ret-35', t] for t in model.t) <= 900.0)

query: The plan retorts nothing but the rich 35-gallon grade and leaves the leaner seams in the ground. That can't be right for resource recovery — shouldn't we be retorting at least 200 units of the 25-gallon grade across the horizon? What's the model seeing that makes it skip it?
expected_pyomo: model.use_lean_grade = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z['ret-25', t] for t in model.t) >= 200.0)

query: Hauling spent shale back to the canyons looks expensive — why does the plan lean so heavily on canyon disposal? Couldn't we hold total canyon-disposal activity under 600 across the horizon? What's keeping the model on that route?
expected_pyomo: model.canyon_disposal_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z['dispose-c', t] for t in model.t) <= 600.0)
